page: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores with hyper-threading, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32 GB per core. It also includes a local 118 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance profile. Single-core CPU performance is moderate, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the average range. However, multi-core CPU performance is weak, as reflected in the stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth benchmarks for large blocks are also positioned in the bottom 25 percent, though the Passmark memory score remains average. Database operations, such as Redis SET operations, and LLM text generation for medium models show weak to poor performance, while static web serving throughput achieves average results.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the x2iedn.xlarge represents a highly specialized resource allocation. It prioritizes massive memory density and local NVMe storage over raw compute power or high-speed memory bandwidth. This makes it a qualitative fit for memory-intensive workloads that do not require heavy parallel CPU processing. Suitable workloads include in-memory databases, caching layers, enterprise applications with large memory footprints, and moderate web serving tasks that benefit from local SSD caching.
description: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance designed for memory-intensive workloads. Powered by a 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8375C processor on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores and 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory, representing a high 32 GB per core ratio. The instance includes a local 118 GB NVMe SSD for high-speed temporary storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show average single-core CPU and memory capabilities, but weak multi-core execution and memory bandwidth. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-effective for applications requiring massive memory capacity relative to compute power, such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-heavy enterprise applications, rather than compute-bound parallel processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 118 GB NVMe SSD, designed for memory-intensive workloads and in-memory databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 118 GB NVMe SSD for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high memory density and local NVMe storage for memory-intensive enterprise applications and caching.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs at 3.5 GHz
128.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 32 GB per physical core
118 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-heavy workloads
